name: Liberia (Republic of Liberia)
summary: Liberia is a West African mixed-legal-system republic combining US-style common-law substantive heritage (via Americo-Liberian settler colonisation rather than direct British colonial inheritance — structurally distinctive in African corpus) with customary-law personal-status jurisdiction operating through Tribal Courts. Family-law framework operates under the Domestic Relations Law (Title 9, Liberian Code of Laws Revised), the Children's Law 2011, the Customary Marriage Law of 1956, and the Equal Rights of Customary Marriage Law 1998. Parental responsibility and child custody are governed by Children's Law 2011 Chapter 5. The Supreme Court of Liberia is the apex court for civil and criminal matters. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Circuit Courts (Family Section), Magistrates' Courts, and Tribal Courts for customary-law matters. Psychology profession is regulated through the Ministry of Health framework. Liberia is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the best-interests-of-the-child principle codified in Children's Law s. 1.4. Liberia is non-Hague Convention.

statutory_framework.title: Children's Law — Parental responsibility and custody
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Children's Law codifying best-interests-of-the-child principle (s. 1.4), parental responsibility, custody, and children's protection provisions.

statutory_framework.citation: Domestic Relations Law Title 9 Liberian Code of Laws Revised
statutory_framework.title: Domestic Relations Law
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal statute on domestic relations including marriage, divorce and custody drawing on US-style common-law tradition.

statutory_framework.citation: Equal Rights of Customary Marriage Law 1998
statutory_framework.title: Equal Rights of Customary Marriage Law
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal statute codifying gender-equality provisions in customary marriages.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of Liberia
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

professional_regulators.name: Ministry of Health, Liberia
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

anonymisation_convention: Liberian family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: American Colonization Society + Americo-Liberian-settlement framework
key_developments.description: American Colonization Society established Americo-Liberian settlement at Cape Mesurado (later Monrovia) 1822 — substantively distinctive globally only-state-founded-by-freed-American-slaves framework. Foundational pre-1847-independence framework affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory and mixed-legal-system-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Liberian independence + earliest-African-independence-in-corpus framework
key_developments.description: Liberia declared independence 26 July 1847 — substantively distinctive globally earliest-African-independence-+-only-state-founded-by-freed-American-slaves framework. Legal-system inheritance via Americo-Liberian settlers from US legal tradition (rather than direct British colonial inheritance) — substantively distinctive within African corpus US-style-common-law-substantive-heritage framework. Foundational substantive 178+ year independent-republic framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Tubman-presidency + Unification-Policy + indigenous-integration framework
key_developments.description: William V.S. Tubman presidency 1944-1971 — substantively distinctive globally Unification-Policy-Americo-Liberian-indigenous-integration framework. Substantive demographic and family-law-implementation transformation affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Customary Marriage Law + customary-law-codification framework
key_developments.description: Customary Marriage Law of 1956 enacted establishing federal statute on customary marriage — substantively significant West African customary-marriage-codification framework. Foundational substantive customary-marriage framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory affecting family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: Liberian Code of Laws Revised + Domestic Relations Law Title 9 framework
key_developments.description: Comprehensive Liberian Code revision 1973 including Domestic Relations Law Title 9 — substantively significant West African US-derivative-domestic-relations-law framework. Foundational substantive domestic-relations-law framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory affecting family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: First-Liberian-Civil-War + Charles-Taylor-+-Doe-Samuel-+-NPFL framework
key_developments.description: First Liberian Civil War December 1989-August 1996 — substantively distinctive West African post-Cold-War-civil-war framework. Estimated 200,000+ deaths and 1 million+ displaced substantively reshaping demographic framework. Subsequent Second Liberian Civil War 1999-2003. Foundational pre-2003-Comprehensive-Peace-Agreement framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Liberia ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child + UNCRC-applicability framework
key_developments.description: Liberia ratified UNCRC 4 June 1993 — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration with subsequent 2011 Children's Law substantive doctrine.

key_developments.title: Equal Rights of Customary Marriage Law + gender-equality-customary-marriage framework
key_developments.description: Equal Rights of Customary Marriage Law 1998 enacted codifying gender-equality provisions in customary marriages — substantively significant West African gender-equality-customary-marriage framework. Substantive family-law-implementation framework affecting subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Children's Law + best-interests-of-the-child-codification framework
key_developments.description: Federal Children's Law 2011 enacted codifying best-interests-of-the-child principle (s. 1.4), parental responsibility, custody, and children's protection provisions. Substantively significant West African children's-law-codification framework. Foundational substantive children's-law framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Boakai election + INCHR + War-and-Economic-Crimes Court framework
key_developments.description: Joseph Boakai (UP) elected President 14 November 2023 substantively distinctive West African democratic-alternation framework ending Weah presidency. Independent National Commission on Human Rights (INCHR) institutional position addressing PA-related issues within Children's Law 2011 + Domestic Relations Law 1973 framework. War and Economic Crimes Court framework 2024 substantively significant transitional-justice framework. Substantively significant West African institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Supreme Court of Liberia + Tribal Courts — best-interests-of-the-child substantive register + Boakai-presidency framework
key_developments.description: Supreme Court of Liberia and Tribal Courts continue to develop best-interests-of-the-child jurisprudence under Children's Law 2011 s. 1.4 + Domestic Relations Law 1973 + Customary Marriage Law 1956 framework in custody disputes within Joseph Boakai (UP) presidency since 22 January 2024. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Liberia operates a structurally distinctive mixed-legal-system framework — US-style common-law substantive heritage (Americo-Liberian settler-inheritance, not direct British colonial) + customary-law personal-status. Unique within the African corpus for US-style common-law substantive tradition.
Independence date 1847 makes Liberia the earliest African independence in the corpus.
Non-Hague Convention status places Liberia in the non-Hague West African cluster.
editorial_notes: Liberia jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 5 to 10 key_developments with full American-Colonization-Society-1822-to-Boakai-presidency trajectory: 1822-American-Colonization-Society-+-Americo-Liberian-settlement + 1847-Liberian-independence-earliest-African + 1944-Tubman-presidency-+-Unification-Policy + 1956-Customary-Marriage-Law + 1973-Liberian-Code-of-Laws-Revised + 1989-First-Liberian-Civil-War + 1993-Liberia-UNCRC-ratification + 1998-Equal-Rights-of-Customary-Marriage-Law + 2011-Children's-Law + 2024-Supreme-Court-+-Tribal-Courts-+-Boakai-presidency.

Mixed-legal-system West Africa (US-style common-law substantive via Americo-Liberian settler-inheritance + customary-law personal-status). Children's Law 2011 + Domestic Relations Law 1973 + Equal Rights of Customary Marriage Law 1998 + non-Hague Convention. Only state founded by freed American slaves globally distinctive + earliest African independence 1847 in corpus.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive best-interests-of-the-child analysis under Children's Law 2011 s. 1.4 + Domestic Relations Law 1973 + Customary Marriage Law 1956 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins West-African + US-style-common-law-distinctive-only-in-African-corpus + earliest-African-independence-1847-globally-distinctive + only-state-founded-by-freed-American-slaves-globally-distinctive + American-Colonization-Society-1822 + Americo-Liberian-settler-heritage + Tubman-presidency-1944-1971-+-Unification-Policy + Customary-Marriage-Law-1956 + Liberian-Code-of-Laws-Revised-1973-Domestic-Relations-Law-Title-9 + First-Liberian-Civil-War-1989-1996-+-Second-Liberian-Civil-War-1999-2003 + Equal-Rights-of-Customary-Marriage-Law-1998 + Children's-Law-2011 + Boakai-UP-2024-presidency + non-Hague-Convention clusters within the corpus.
